mission.title: Install and Configure ClickHouse
mission.description: ClickHouse is a real-time analytics database management system designed for generating analytical data reports quickly and efficiently. It is ideal for applications requiring high-performance data processing and analytics. Key trade-offs include the need for careful schema design and potential complexity in managing distributed setups.

steps.title: Add ClickHouse Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the ClickHouse Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install ClickHouse using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add clickhouse https://clickhouse.github.io/clickhouse-operator

helm repo update

```

> This adds the official ClickHouse operator repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install ClickHouse Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the ClickHouse operator in the specified namespace, which manages ClickHouse clusters.

```bash

kubectl create namespace clickhouse

helm install clickhouse-operator clickhouse/clickhouse-operator --namespace clickhouse --version 0.24.0

```

> Creates a namespace for ClickHouse.
> Installs the ClickHouse operator with a specific version.

steps.title: Deploy ClickHouse Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a ClickHouse cluster custom resource (CR) that the operator will manage.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: clickhouse.altinity.com/v1
kind: ClickHouseCluster
metadata:
  name: example
  namespace: clickhouse
spec:
  configuration:
    clusters:
      - name: example
        layout:
          shardsCount: 1
          replicasCount: 1
EOF

```

> Creates a simple ClickHouse cluster with one shard and one replica.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: clickhouse.altinity.com/v1
kind: ClickHouseCluster
metadata:
  name: example
  namespace: clickhouse
spec:
  configuration:
    clusters:
      - name: example
        layout:
          shardsCount: 1
          replicasCount: 1
EOF

steps.title: Verify ClickHouse Cluster Status
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the ClickHouse cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get clickhouseclusters -n clickhouse

kubectl logs -l app=clickhouse-operator -n clickhouse

```

> Lists ClickHouse clusters and their statuses.
> Fetches logs from the ClickHouse operator for troubleshooting.

uninstall.title: Uninstall ClickHouse Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the ClickHouse operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the ClickHouse cluster and all its data.

```bash

helm uninstall clickhouse-operator --namespace clickhouse

kubectl delete namespace clickhouse

```

> Uninstalls the ClickHouse operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade ClickHouse Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step will upgrade the ClickHouse operator to a newer version.

```bash

kubectl get clickhouseclusters -n clickhouse -o json > clickhouse-backup.json

helm upgrade clickhouse-operator clickhouse/clickhouse-operator --namespace clickhouse --version 0.25.0

kubectl get clickhouseclusters -n clickhouse

```

> Backs up the current ClickHouse cluster configuration.
> Upgrades the ClickHouse operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the ClickHouse cluster is still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get clickhouseclusters -n clickhouse -o json > clickhouse-backup.json

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create ClickHouse cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the operator is not running or if there are configuration issues.

**Fix:**
Check the operator logs with 'kubectl logs -l app=clickhouse-operator -n clickhouse' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: ClickHouse cluster is not responding
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the pods are not running or are in a crash loop.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl get pods -n clickhouse' to check pod status and 'kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n clickhouse' for detailed error messages.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the ClickHouse pods.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cluster has sufficient CPU and memory resources available for ClickHouse.

troubleshooting.title: ClickHouse operator not found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the operator was not installed correctly or the namespace is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the installation with 'kubectl get pods -n clickhouse' and ensure the namespace is correct.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new SQL functions and improved performance for distributed queries.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old configuration format for clusters.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v25.x, ensure to update your cluster configuration to the new format.

resolution.summary: You should see the ClickHouse cluster listed with 'kubectl get clickhouseclusters -n clickhouse', and the status should be 'Running'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: clickhouse.altinity.com/v1
kind: ClickHouseCluster
metadata:
  name: example
  namespace: clickhouse
spec:
  configuration:
    clusters:
      - name: example
        layout:
          shardsCount: 1
          replicasCount: 1
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
